notes: So what I've done for this session is make notes on the book chapters I've read for The pragmatic programmer. I touched on Software Entropy & the Stone Soup and Boiled Frogs chapters. I think even holistically what I've gained from the book is the importance of being responsible and being able to stand behind the decisions you choose to take on. Also the ability and humility to admit and manup when you are wrong, or just simply admitting that you don't know something. I liked the broken windows chapter because it is a thing whereby when you look back you don't know where it all went wrong, and I have to start solving the issues that I see as they happen, as I note them. Because these tiny things add up to affect the overall psychology of the project. If they don't care why would I? This chapter I just read touches on how I have to be the catalyst for the change I wish to see, bring solutions and gently suggest them. Bureaucracy gets in the way often times because everyone is thinking of themselves even though the work is for the collective good.
